title: Playtest Closure, Release Gates, Rollback, Recovery, and Production Defense · Runtime Lens: Crash Recovery
summary: A polished demo becomes release by copying the latest executable; no one owns accessibility findings, performance budgets, package provenance, crash recovery, rollback, or support. Predict player-visible behavior, state, units, timing, input, rendering, feedback, failure, accessibility, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the crash recovery increment in production game release and defense dossier. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theo…
objectives: Preserve bounded diagnostics and settings backups, recover corrupted local state safely, and provide accessible support instructions.
Gate an immutable identified artifact on correctness, replay, accessibility, performance, portability, security, licenses, and clean launch.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

content.text: A worked example Asteroids team handles case 4631 during a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling. Build deterministic Python evidence to defend an immutable game release across player value, accessibility, determinism, performance, portability, security, rollback, recovery, and owned residual risk; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native display, device, audio, file, package, and operating-system effects; then inject a 400 millisecond frame stall and prove the update budget prevents a spiral of death. A defensible solution applies Crash Recovery, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to production game release and defense dossier.

content.code:
# Worked focus: asteroids-crash-recovery — Preserve bounded diagnostics and settings backups, recover corrupted local state safely, and provide accessible support instructions.
# Evidence: asteroids-worked-asteroids-release-recovery-defense-2
# Competency: asteroids-worked-asteroids-release-recovery-defense-2.
# Case 7417: a headless continuous-integration replay.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state.
def worked_asteroids_release_recovery_defense_2(correctness: bool = True, accessibility: bool = True, performance: bool = True, portability: bool = True, security: bool = True, rollback: bool = True, recovery: bool = True, residual_risk_owned: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "playerworked2",
        "seed7417",
        "input2dekrow",
        "frameworked27417",
        "state7417worked2",
        "playtest2dekrow7417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    gates = (correctness, accessibility, performance, portability, security, rollback, recovery)
    passed = sum(1 for gate in gates if gate)
    if not correctness or not accessibility:
        return False, passed, "player-quality"
    if not performance or not portability or not security:
        return False, passed, "release-quality"
    if not rollback or not recovery:
        return False, passed, "recovery-gap"
    if not residual_risk_owned:
        return False, passed, "unowned-risk"
    assert passed == len(gates)
    return True, passed, "production-game-defense"

stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A theory Asteroids team handles case 1202 during a low-power integrated-GPU frame-budget trial. Build deterministic Python evidence to defend an immutable game release across player value, accessibility, determinism, performance, portability, security, rollback, recovery, and owned residual risk; keep every critical cue available through more than color or sound and every action remappable without timing precision; then replace an audio-only collision cue with synchronized text, shape, and optional sound evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Preserve bounded diagnostics and settings backups, recover corrupted local state safely, and provide accessible support instructions.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Restarting the game is an adequate recovery plan for every failure.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Preserve bounded diagnostics and settings backups, recover corrupted local state safely, and provide accessible support instructions. Artifact under review: production game release and defense dossier.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Crash Recovery, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Crash Recovery record says production game release and defense dossier looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the inspect crash recovery evidence boundary for Crash Recovery.

For inspect crash recovery evidence, run one convenient high-frame-rate happy path, omit revision, seed, input and time trace, units, seams, device changes, entity reconciliation, multichannel cues, accessibility settings, failures, recovery, and desktop transfer, then accept a playable animation as Asteroids proof. This does not satisfy Crash Recovery for production game release and defense dossier.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Crash Recovery model · Theory Crash Recovery
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Crash Recovery: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory Asteroids team handles case 2821 during a headless continuous-integration replay. Build deterministic Python evidence to defend an immutable game release across player value, accessibility, determinism, performance, portability, security, rollback, recovery, and owned residual risk; keep every critical cue available through more than color or sound and every action remappable without timing precision; then lose window focus while thrust and fire are held, then prove both actions release safely. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Preserve bounded diagnostics and settings backups, recover corrupted local state safely, and provide accessible support instructions.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Crash Recovery model · Theory Crash Recovery.
steps.why: Retrieving Crash Recovery through the concrete production game release and defense dossier case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Crash Recovery model · Theory Crash Recovery, keep Crash Recovery connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Preserve bounded diagnostics and settings backups, recover corrupted local state safely, and provide accessible support instructions.

hints: For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Crash Recovery model · Theory Crash Recovery, name the player, revision, runtime, seed, input, time, coordinate, state, entity, collision, render, cue, accessibility, package, release, and desktop-transfer boundary, Crash Recovery precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Crash Recovery model · Theory Crash Recovery, trace this concrete input and predict event, intent, simulation, collision, cleanup, rendering, cue, accessibility, failure, pause, rollback, recovery, and desktop evidence before reviewing it: A theory Asteroids team handles case 2821 during a headless continuous-integration replay. Build deterministic Python evidence to defend an immutable game release across player value, accessibility, determinism, performance, portability, security, rollback, recovery, and owned residual risk; keep every critical cue available through more than color or sound and every action remappable without timing precision; then lose window focus while thrust and fire are held, then prove both actions release safely. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Preserve bounded diagnostics and settings backups, recover corrupted local state safely, and provide accessible support instructions.

For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Crash Recovery model · Theory Crash Recovery, challenge the documented misconception with changed-frame-rate, stall, seam, input, device, collision, cue, accessibility, save, package, failure, recovery, and controlled desktop-transfer evidence: Restarting the game is an adequate recovery plan for every failure.
